meta.title: Facilitators Content Reference
meta.subtitle: Segment 1 — Weeks 1–4: Talks, Decks, Activities & Daily Flow
meta.purpose: Single staff reference for every program day: presentation decks (Deck 01 & Deck 02), session flow, activities, assessments, mentor coaching focus, and gate milestones. Week 1 is synced from published Playbook content; Weeks 2–4 are facilitator drafts until Content Studio publish.

weeks.title: Ambition & Purpose
weeks.milestoneObjective: Establish personal ambition, purpose, and venture identity foundations aligned with the 40-hour Ambition Review gate.
weeks.businessPlanChapter: Business Plan Chapter 1 — Venture Identity & Direction

days.title: Day 1 — Stories, Identity & Squad Formation
days.theme: Discover Yourself — AIA, Agency, SPIKE, and Building My Future
learningObjectives: Understand the AIA, Agency, and SPIKE stories that frame the incubator experience
Articulate personal ambition, impact, values, future self, and career direction
Complete identity builders in Venture Blueprint and sync to Venture Portfolio
Form research squads, build dream boards, and sign squad charters

decks.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — Stories That Frame SPIKE

slides.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — Opening
slides.summary: Today we begin with three stories: 1. The AIA Story — why protection matters 2. The Agency Story — how financial entrepreneurs build 3. The SPIKE Story — how you become a venture builder These stories are not background …
slides.facilitatorNotes: Good morning everyone. Welcome to SPIKE. Today is not simply the start of an internship. It is the beginning of a journey that may shape your career, your business, and perhaps even your life. Over the next several weeks, you will learn about financial services, entrepreneurship, leadership, teamwork, and personal growth. But more importantly, you will discover something much more valuable... You …
discussionQuestions: Which story are you most curious to hear today?
What does 'venture builder' mean to you right now — before we define it?

slides.title: The AIA Story
slides.summary: AIA was founded in Shanghai in 1919 with a single conviction: people deserve financial protection when life does not go as planned. Today, AIA is the largest pan-Asian life insurance group — a company built on trust, dis…
slides.facilitatorNotes: Before we talk about SPIKE... I'd like you to understand the organization that made this opportunity possible. AIA is not simply an insurance company. We exist for one purpose: Helping people live healthier, longer, better lives. Those six words shape every decision we make. When someone joins AIA, they aren't simply joining a company. They're joining a mission. During SPIKE, you'll discover that …
discussionQuestions: When have you seen a family struggle because they lacked financial protection?
How does AIA's mission connect to your personal why?

slides.title: The Agency Story
slides.summary: The agency model is not a job — it is a franchise of entrepreneurship. Financial advisors build books of business. Agency builders recruit, develop, and lead teams. Unit Managers and Agency Directors invest in people the…
slides.facilitatorNotes: What do you see?" People answer: A family. Parents. Children. Happiness. Play. Then ask: "What don't you see?" Silence. Then answer: You don't see the risks they're protected from. Powerful. Think about this... Every family has dreams. A first home. A child's education. Retirement. Starting a business. But every dream faces risk. Illness. Accidents. Disability. Loss of income. At AIA, we race agai…
discussionQuestions: What is the difference between having a job and building a venture?
Who is an agency leader you admire — and what did they build?

slides.title: The SPIKE Story
slides.summary: SPIKE = Startup Business Incubator for Knowledge Entrepreneurs. You are not interns filling hours — you are venture builders creating: • Venture Portfolio™ (your personal brand and proof) • Venture Blueprint™ (your 3-yea…
slides.facilitatorNotes: "How long does it take to build trust?" People answer. Then "Years." Pause. "Sometimes generations." Pause. "That's exactly what AIA has spent decades doing. Trust is earned over time. AIA Philippines traces its roots back to 1947, when it was established as Philam Life. For generations, Filipino families have trusted the company to help them prepare for life's uncertainties. Today, AIA Philippine…
discussionQuestions: Which output system — Portfolio or Business Plan — feels most unfamiliar?
What milestone at Hour 200 would make you proud?

slides.title: Program Architecture & Squads
slides.summary: Segment 1 (200 hours) → Proof of Concept Venture Board. Research Squads (3–4): market intelligence and collaborative problem solving. Advisory Dyads: roleplay, mock client meetings, pitch refinement. Agency Teams: busine…
slides.facilitatorNotes: Many people believe insurance is the product. It isn't. The product is peace of mind. Great financial advisors educate. They listen. They ask questions. They guide families through important decisions. Insurance simply becomes one of the tools that supports those decisions. That is why we call this profession financial advising—not selling.
discussionQuestions: What strength will you bring to your research squad?
What do you want from your mentor in Week 1?

slides.title: Deck 01 Close — Transition to Building My Future
slides.summary: You have heard three stories: • AIA protects what matters • The agency model rewards builders • SPIKE turns you into a financial entrepreneur Next: Program Coach Deck 02 — Building My Future. You will define Ambition, Im…
slides.facilitatorNotes: Every Agency Director... Every MDRT member... Every successful leader... Every entrepreneur... began exactly where you are today. With curiosity. None of them started with all the answers. They learned. They practiced. They served. And eventually, they built careers that changed both their own lives and the lives of others. SPIKE exists to help you begin that same journey. Not by telling you what …
discussionQuestions: What is one word that captures how you feel after these three stories?
What question do you want answered about your own future today?

slides.title: Slide 7
slides.facilitatorNotes: Opening (Don't Read the Slide) Walk toward the audience. Pause. Then ask: How many of you have spent at least 15 years in school? (Wait for hands.) Smile. That's almost everyone. Continue. In those 15 years... you've learned mathematics... science... English... research... presentations... examinations... and many other subjects. Pause. Those are all important. The Question Then ask: But let me as…

slides.title: Slide 8
slides.facilitatorNotes: Opening Walk toward the audience. Pause. Smile. Ask the room. When you hear the word "internship"... what comes to mind? Wait. Write their answers if using a whiteboard. Typical responses: OJT Office work Photocopying Encoding Coffee Assisting Learning Following instructions Smile. Those are all valid answers. Pause. The Reality For many students... an internship is simply about completing the req…

slides.title: Slide 10
slides.facilitatorNotes: Everything you create in SPIKE... Pause. doesn't disappear after Friday. Pause. It becomes part of your professional identity. Pause. This portfolio grows with you... through research... through clients... through leadership... through recruitment... through becoming an Agency Director. Now the audience is imagining their future , not reading bullets.

decks.title: Program Coach Deck 02 — Building My Future

slides.title: Slide 1
slides.facilitatorNotes: The greatest entrepreneurs never succeed alone. Pause. They have mentors. Pause. They have partners. Pause. They have teammates. Pause. They have technology that helps them think better. Pause. SPIKE gives you all four.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.facilitatorNotes: One thing you'll notice throughout SPIKE is that we don't use AI to think for you. (Pause.) We use AI to help you think better. Continue The ideas... The dreams... The purpose... The mission... The venture... They all come from you. (Pause.) AI simply helps you sharpen them. Point to the Laptop Imagine you're writing your Purpose Statement. You already know what you want to say. But maybe you're n…

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.facilitatorNotes: We will be using this tool.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.facilitatorNotes: Speaker: This will be your Venture Blueprint. "By the end of today, every one of you will have your own Venture Blueprint.”

slides.title: Slide 6
slides.facilitatorNotes: Today, we're going to build all of these.

slides.title: Slide 7
slides.facilitatorNotes: Your Purpose "You've just refined your ambition." (Pause.) "Now we're going one level deeper." "Ambition answers the question: What do I want to achieve?" (Pause.) "Purpose answers a different question." (Pause.) "Why does this venture deserve to exist?" "A successful venture isn't built around a product." "It begins with a problem worth solving and people worth serving." "Don't worry about writin…

slides.title: Slide 8
slides.facilitatorNotes: Instead of giving them answers, walk around the room asking questions like: "Who are you trying to help?" "What frustrates them today?" "Why does this matter to you personally?" "If you could solve only one problem, what would it be?" "Why should anyone care that your venture exists?" Transition to the Next Slide (Share) As the timer ends: "Your first draft doesn't have to be perfect." "Now let's …

slides.title: Slide 9
slides.facilitatorNotes: Prompt Imagine your life five years from now. Where are you? Who are you helping? What does success look like? Large inspirational image. 20 minutes.

sessionFlow.activity: Program Coach Deck 01 — AIA, Agency, SPIKE Stories
sessionFlow.notes: Deliver all six Deck 01 slides with stories, not bullet reads. Use discussion questions on slides 2–4.

sessionFlow.activity: Program Coach Deck 02 — Building My Future (overview + Ambition + Impact)
sessionFlow.notes: Slides 1–3. Transition directly into ambition and impact builders.
Individual work with mentor circulation. Debrief each activity using activity guide questions.
Mentors check squad roster and preview afternoon flow.

sessionFlow.activity: Deck 02 — Future Self + Career Direction slides + builders
sessionFlow.notes: Slides 5–6 then activities. Dyad discussion for career direction.
Squads form visually then confirm membership in app. Gallery walk at 2:55.
Digital signatures required. Program Coach and mentor sign-off.
Program Coach uses observation form and venture identity rubric. Participation assessment in app.
Participant reflection survey. Mentors complete debrief reflection form with program coach.

activities.title: Ambition Builder — Venture Coach™
instructions: Program Coach introduces Ambition after Deck 02 slide 2.
Participants open Venture Blueprint → Day 1 Builders → My Ambition.
Venture Coach™ guides a conversation — participants respond in their own words.
Coach generates a draft ambition statement; participant edits until authentic.
Submit when clarity score is acceptable or your program coach calls time.
Mentors circulate — listen for copied language vs personal voice.
outputs: Submitted ambition statement in Venture Blueprint
debriefQuestions: Who heard something surprising in a squad mate's ambition?
What made your ambition statement feel true — not performative?
How does your ambition connect to the AIA Story from Deck 01?

activities.title: Impact Builder — Venture Coach™
instructions: Facilitate immediately after ambition debrief.
Open Impact builder in Venture Coach™.
Participants name audience + transformation in guided prompts.
Review draft impact statement for specificity.
Submit and confirm sync to Portfolio Identity section.
outputs: Business Plan Chapter 1 — purpose paragraph seed
debriefQuestions: Who is the specific audience in your impact statement?
How does impact differ from ambition in your own words?
Where could your impact show up in a real client conversation?

activities.title: Values Profile Builder
instructions: Present Values slide from Deck 02.
Participants select values that resonate from the card deck.
Rank top three — forced prioritization.
Write one sentence on how top value guides decisions.
Submit values profile to Blueprint and Portfolio.
debriefQuestions: Which value was hardest to rank — and why?
Share one value your squad shares and one where you differ.
How will your values appear in your squad charter?

activities.title: Future Self Narrative Builder
instructions: Program Coach reads exemplar 3-year narrative (Deck 02 slide 5).
Participants build future self through interactive timeline in Venture Coach™.
Encourage present-tense narrative — 'I am' not 'I will'.
Generate summary for portfolio and mentor snapshot.
Mentors note confidence and specificity during circulation.
materials: Venture Blueprint app — Future Self builder
debriefQuestions: What detail in your future self narrative felt most real?
What fear showed up while writing — and did you name it?
How does future self connect to your ambition statement?

activities.title: Career Direction Explorer
instructions: Present both career tracks without prescribing either.
Participants explore Venture Direction builder — no final lock required on Day 1.
Capture initial preference and one reason.
Discuss with dyad partner for 5 minutes.
Save exploration state to Blueprint Career Track module.
materials: Venture Blueprint app — Venture Direction builder
Agency Builder vs Specialist Consultant comparison (Deck 02 slide 6)
outputs: Career track note for mentor observation
debriefQuestions: Which track energized you — and what evidence supports that?
What question do you still need answered before committing?
How does career direction connect to your future self narrative?

activities.title: Dream Board Studio
instructions: Squads gather for visual dream board creation.
Each member adds lifestyle, family, career, and financial goal images.
Write captions connecting visuals to ambition and future self.
Export 16:9 collage for Venture Portfolio Dream Board section.
Squad shares one board highlight in gallery walk (2 min per squad).
materials: Dream Board Studio in Venture Blueprint
Optional: poster boards, markers, printed images
debriefQuestions: Which image on your board represents your ambition best?
Where did squad members' dreams align or diverge?
How does your dream board support your impact statement?

activities.title: Research Squad Formation
instructions: Program Coach announces squad assignments or guides self-selection by market interest.
Participants complete Squad Formation builder — rank research market preferences.
Confirm squad roster and mentor assignment in app.
Introduce squad members and exchange contact details.
Mentor joins each squad for 5-minute introduction.
debriefQuestions: What strength does each squad member bring?
What research market excites your squad most?
How will your squad communicate between sessions?

activities.title: Squad Charter Builder
instructions: Assign squad roles for Week 1 rotation.
Co-create mission statement linking squad vision to cohort theme.
Document meeting cadence, communication channel, and conflict norms.
Each member signs charter digitally in app.
Program Coach and mentor sign-off before session close.
materials: Squad Charter Builder in Venture Blueprint
Role description handout: Lead, Scribe, Presenter, Analyst
debriefQuestions: What norm will keep your squad effective under time pressure?
Who will present squad outputs at the Day 5 review?
How does your charter reflect shared values?

prepChecklist: Load Program Coach Deck 01 and Deck 02 — confirm speaker notes visible in program coach view
Confirm squad assignments and mentor pairings in formation admin
Test Venture Blueprint Day 1 Builders access for all participants
Print or share evaluation templates: participation rubric, venture identity rubric, program coach observation form
Brief mentors on Day 1 mentor guide and observation areas
Prepare dream board materials (digital studio + optional physical supplies)
debriefQuestions: Which squad dream boards showed strongest alignment between personal ambition and venture thesis?
Where did participants struggle to distinguish entrepreneurship from traditional employment?
Which identity builder produced the most authentic voice — and which produced the most generic answers?
What coaching will mentors provide before Day 2 industry immersion?
Are all Day 1 deliverables visible in Venture Portfolio for every participant?
commonPitfalls: Rushing Deck 01 stories — participants need emotional frame before builders
Reading slides instead of telling AIA and agency stories
Skipping debrief questions after each builder activity
Treating identity builders as forms to rush through
Allowing squad charter without assigned roles or digital signatures
coachingTips: Program Coaches and mentors use the same language: ambition, impact, values, future self, career direction
Link every activity output to Portfolio Identity and Business Plan Chapter 1
Use Week 1 Journey panel in Venture Portfolio as live progress check
Flag participants with observation form 'needs follow-up' for mentor priority on Day 2

mentorGuide.title: Mentor Guide — Day 1: Identity & Purpose
mentorGuide.coachingObjective: Connect the SPIKE opportunity to each participant's personal ambition. Help them find authentic voice in identity builders — not perfect words.

timing.preSession: 15 min — review squad roster, read Program Coach Deck 01/02 speaker notes, print observation form
timing.duringSession: Circulate during builders and squad activities; 5 min intro per squad
timing.postSession: 20 min — complete observation forms and participant snapshots

discussionQuestions: Why did you join SPIKE?
What attracted you to this opportunity?
What future are you hoping to create?
What concerns do you have about the entrepreneurship path?
What part of today resonated most — AIA story, agency story, or your own ambition?
Say your ambition in one sentence — I want to hear it in your words.
Who do you want to help — and why them?
What value will you never compromise?

lookFor: Speaks up in squad without prompting

warningSigns: Generic answers with no personal detail

lookFor: Connects AIA mission to personal why
warningSigns: No connection between ambition and impact
Copied phrases from slides or peers

expectedOutputs: Ambition, impact, values, future self, and career direction statements
Dream board collage in Venture Portfolio
Squad membership and signed squad charter
Day 1 program coach and mentor evaluation observations

days.title: Day 2 — Discover The Industry
learningObjectives: Map the financial services and insurance landscape in the Philippines
Connect industry opportunity to personal ambition from Day 1
Prepare for practitioner interviews and squad research
Draft opportunity reflection for portfolio and business plan

decks.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — The Financial Services Landscape

slides.title: Day 2 Opening — From Identity to Industry
slides.summary: Yesterday you defined who you are becoming. Today we ask: what industry will you build in? Financial services is one of the largest, most stable, and most impact-driven sectors in the Philippines.
slides.facilitatorNotes: Bridge from Day 1 identity. Reference participant ambition statements. 3 min.
discussionQuestions: How does your ambition connect to an industry — not just a job?

slides.title: Financial Services Ecosystem
slides.summary: Banks · Insurance · Investments · Financial Planning · Fintech Protection products (life, health, VUL) fund education, retirement, and legacy. Advisors and agency builders are entrepreneurs within this ecosystem.
slides.facilitatorNotes: Use local examples. Avoid product pitch — this is landscape. 6 min.
discussionQuestions: Which part of the ecosystem interests you most?

slides.title: Insurance & Protection Story
slides.summary: Insurance is not a transaction — it is a promise. AIA's protection mission: help people live Healthier, Longer, Better Lives. The opportunity: millions of Filipinos are underinsured. Financial entrepreneurs close the pro…
slides.facilitatorNotes: Connect to AIA Day 1 story. 5 min.
discussionQuestions: Would you personally buy protection today? Why or why not?

slides.title: Career Opportunities in Financial Entrepreneurship
slides.summary: Advisor → Unit Manager → Agency Director Specialist Consultant → Practice Owner SPIKE prepares you for both agency building and specialist consulting paths.
slides.facilitatorNotes: Link to Day 1 career direction exploration. 4 min.
discussionQuestions: Where do you see yourself adding value in this industry?

slides.title: Deck 01 Close — Industry Immersion Begins
slides.summary: Next: Deck 02 — Connecting Industry to Your Ambition. Then: practitioner interviews, squad debrief, and research plan.
discussionQuestions: What do you want to learn from a practitioner today?

decks.title: Program Coach Deck 02 — Connecting Industry to Your Ambition

slides.title: Ambition Meets Industry
slides.summary: Your ambition statement is not abstract — it lives in a real market. Today you will: • Interview or observe a practitioner • Capture industry insights for your squad • Draft your opportunity reflection
slides.facilitatorNotes: Show example connection: ambition → industry niche. 4 min.
discussionQuestions: Read your ambition — what industry segment fits?

slides.title: Interview & Observation Protocol
slides.summary: Prepare 5 questions before you speak to a practitioner. Listen for: client problems, career path, daily habits, industry changes. Capture quotes — your business plan will use this evidence.
discussionQuestions: What is one question you are afraid to ask?

slides.title: Squad Research Plan
slides.summary: Squads will research a market segment this week. Document: target segment, key questions, interview schedule, roles. Lead presents research plan at debrief.
discussionQuestions: What segment will your squad investigate?

slides.title: Day 2 Deliverables
slides.summary: ✓ Interview notes ✓ Industry insights (squad) ✓ Research plan ✓ Opportunity reflection → Portfolio
discussionQuestions: What is one action before Day 3 market observation?

sessionFlow.notes: Tell stories, not bullet reads.
Launch interview activity.
Mentors circulate with observation form.
Compile squad insights.
Close with Day 2 deliverables.

activities.title: Industry Immersion Session
instructions: Program Coach delivers Deck 01 with discussion after slides 2–4.
Participants take structured notes in interview worksheet.
Facilitate Q&A on financial services landscape.
debriefQuestions: What surprised you about the industry today?
What assumption from yesterday changed?
How does protection connect to your impact statement?

activities.title: Practitioner Interview / Observation
instructions: Pairs or triads interview a practitioner, mentor, or program coach guest.
Use worksheet prompts — minimum 3 substantive answers.
Record key quotes for business plan evidence.
debriefQuestions: What quote will you remember from today?
What career path did the practitioner describe?
What habit could you adopt this week?

activities.title: Squad Industry Debrief
instructions: Squads share top 3 industry insights.
Identify overlap with research market segment.
Assign scribe to compile squad insight summary.
debriefQuestions: Where did squad insights align or diverge?
What is your squad's research priority for Week 1?
Who presents at Day 3 persona workshop?

activities.title: Opportunity Reflection
instructions: Individual reflection linking industry to Day 1 ambition.
Complete worksheet question 3 and survey.
Sync to Portfolio market intelligence section.
debriefQuestions: How did today change your 3-year picture?
What opportunity excites you most?
What concern remains about this industry?

prepChecklist: Confirm practitioner guests or mentor stand-ins
Load Deck 01 and Deck 02 with speaker notes
Print interview worksheets
Brief mentors on observation form
debriefQuestions: Which squads connected industry to ambition most clearly?
Where did participants show curiosity vs skepticism?
What coaching is needed before Day 3?
commonPitfalls: Generic industry notes without personal connection
coachingTips: Link every insight back to Day 1 ambition
Push for named practitioners and real quotes

mentorGuide.title: Mentor Guide — Day 2: Opportunity
mentorGuide.coachingObjective: Connect industry opportunity to personal goals from Day 1.

timing.preSession: 10 min — review deck speaker notes and observation form
timing.duringSession: Circulate during squad activities; coach individuals at transitions
timing.postSession: 15 min — complete observation forms and coaching notes

discussionQuestions: What surprised you about the industry?
What assumptions changed today?
Would you personally buy insurance today? Why?
Where do you see opportunity?

days.title: Day 3 — Discover The Market
learningObjectives: Identify customer problems through observation and conversation
Build evidence-based customer personas
Map unmet needs to venture opportunity
Launch squad needs-analysis research plan

decks.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — Problems, Needs & Segments

slides.title: Day 3 — Customer Discovery
slides.summary: Entrepreneurs do not start with products. They start with problems. Today: observe, listen, persona — then opportunity.
discussionQuestions: What customer did you think about on the way in today?

slides.title: Problems Before Solutions
slides.summary: Great ventures solve painful problems. Listen for: frequency, intensity, willingness to pay for relief. Avoid solution-first thinking until problems are clear.
slides.facilitatorNotes: Use squad examples from Day 2 research plan. 5 min.
discussionQuestions: What problem did you hear repeatedly yesterday?

slides.title: Customer Segments
slides.summary: Demographics + psychographics + behavior. Examples: young parents, freelancers, SME owners, OFW families. Your squad segment should match your research market.
slides.facilitatorNotes: Connect to squad formation Day 1. 5 min.
discussionQuestions: Who is most affected by the problems you identified?

slides.title: Needs Analysis Mindset
slides.summary: Needs analysis is not a form — it is a conversation. SPIKE squads conduct real surveys this week. Today you design the persona that guides those conversations.
slides.facilitatorNotes: Preview survey activity in app. 4 min.
discussionQuestions: What question will you ask in your first survey?

slides.title: Deck 01 Close
slides.summary: Next: Deck 02 — Persona Workshop. Then: market observation and squad presentations.
discussionQuestions: What will you observe in the next hour?

decks.title: Program Coach Deck 02 — Venture Studio (Interactive)

slides.title: Venture Studio — Interactive Workshop
slides.summary: Deck 02 is now an in-app interactive module. Open Venture Studio from the Playbook Day 3 session. Squads work through five guided steps with coach feedback and an evidence library. Facilitators: use “Open for projection”…
slides.facilitatorNotes: Direct squads to Playbook → Day 3 → Enter Venture Studio. Use present mode for projection. 3 min orientation.
discussionQuestions: What evidence will your squad capture in the field today?

sessionFlow.notes: Deck 02 is interactive — project Venture Studio for squads.
Safety briefing first.
Push for evidence.
Use persona rubric.

activities.title: Market Observation Walk
instructions: Squads conduct structured observation in assigned area.
Capture verbatim quotes and behaviors.
No selling — listen and learn only.
debriefQuestions: What exact words did customers use?
What surprised you in the field?
What problem appeared most urgent?

activities.title: Customer Persona Workshop (Venture Studio)
instructions: Open Venture Studio from the Day 3 Playbook session.
Complete all five steps as a squad — evidence library boosts your coach score.
Finish with the Venture Opportunity Report (auto-saves to your squad workspace).
debriefQuestions: Is your persona based on evidence or assumption?
What is the persona's biggest unmet need?
How does persona connect to your impact statement?

activities.title: Squad Research Planning
instructions: Finalize needs-analysis survey questions (5–8).
Assign survey roles and targets for Week 1.
Set completion goal before Day 5.
debriefQuestions: How many surveys will your squad complete this week?
What could bias your results?
How will you share findings in portfolio?

activities.title: Squad Persona Presentations
instructions: Each squad presents persona + opportunity hypothesis.
Program Coach scores with persona rubric.
Mentors note coaching opportunities.
debriefQuestions: Which persona was most convincing — and why?
What feedback will improve your persona?
What opportunity will you test next?

prepChecklist: Confirm observation locations and permissions
Load persona exemplar
Brief mentors on empathy coaching
debriefQuestions: Which personas lacked evidence?
What problems appeared across squads?
Are survey plans realistic?
coachingTips: Demand verbatim quotes
Link persona to Day 1 impact audience

mentorGuide.title: Mentor Guide — Day 3: Customer

timing.preSession: 10 min — review deck speaker notes and observation form
timing.duringSession: Circulate during squad activities; coach individuals at transitions
timing.postSession: 15 min — complete observation forms and coaching notes

discussionQuestions: What concerns did people talk about?
What needs appeared repeatedly?
What surprised you?
What opportunities did you discover?

days.title: Day 4 — Financial Entrepreneurship
learningObjectives: Distinguish advisor, practice owner, and agency builder paths
Draft Unified Venture Proposition through Venture Design Studio
Align UVP to career direction
Prepare for Day 5 venture commitment

decks.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — Advisor vs Entrepreneur

slides.title: Day 4 — Financial Entrepreneurship
discussionQuestions: What kind of entrepreneur do you want to become?

slides.title: Advisor vs Entrepreneur
discussionQuestions: Where have you been an advisor vs entrepreneur in your life?

slides.title: Agency Builder Path
discussionQuestions: What excites you about building a team?

slides.title: Specialist Consultant Path
discussionQuestions: What niche could you own in 3 years?

slides.title: Deck 01 Close
discussionQuestions: Which path will you explore first today?

decks.title: Program Coach Deck 02 — Venture Design Studio (Interactive)

slides.title: Venture Design Studio — Interactive Workshop
slides.summary: Deck 02 is now an in-app interactive module. Open Venture Design Studio from the Playbook Day 4 session. Squads work through five guided steps with coach feedback and FEC canvas sync. Facilitators: use “Open for projecti…
slides.facilitatorNotes: Direct squads to Playbook → Day 4 → Enter Venture Design Studio. Use coach projection mode for screen display. 3 min orientation.
discussionQuestions: Which FEC engine will your squad prioritize first?

sessionFlow.activity: Deck 01 intro + Venture Design Studio launch
sessionFlow.notes: Deck 02 is in-app — open from Playbook, not slides.
Honest exploration.
Target: drafted UVP (Step 3) per squad.
Coach UVP clarity, not FEC completion %.

activities.title: Career Track Exploration Clinic
instructions: Review Agency Builder vs Specialist Consultant in Venture Coach.
Dyads discuss evidence for each path.
Update career direction checkpoint — exploration or preference.
debriefQuestions: Which pathway energizes you today?
What evidence supports that?
What would change your mind?

activities.title: FE Canvas Workshop
instructions: Open Venture Design Studio from Playbook Day 4 Session 1.
Complete all five Venture Design Studio steps — focus on Step 3 UVP synthesis.
Generate Portfolio View, then tap Save to Portfolio (UVP, venture name, FEC snapshot). No presentations yet.
materials: Venture Design Studio in Playbook (replaces Deck 02)
debriefQuestions: What engine was hardest?
Does canvas match your ambition?
What will you finish before Day 5?

activities.title: Canvas Peer Review
instructions: Dyads exchange canvas summaries.
Give one strength and one gap feedback.
Revise strategy statement if needed.
debriefQuestions: What feedback was most useful?
What gap will you close this week?
How does canvas connect to your persona?

activities.title: Canvas Snapshot Presentations
instructions: Volunteers share 2-minute canvas snapshot.
Program Coach uses canvas rubric.
Mentors note coaching priorities for Day 5.
debriefQuestions: Which presentation showed clearest venture model?
What will you add before Day 5?
How does canvas support your future self?

prepChecklist: Test Venture Design Studio access
Load Deck 01
Prepare FEC projection (blank + full exemplar) for UVP overview
debriefQuestions: Who drafted a clear UVP?
Which path drew most energy?
Day 5 presentation prep?
commonPitfalls: Chasing FEC completion instead of UVP
coachingTips: Use proposition gaps as coaching prompts
Link UVP to persona and ambition from earlier days

mentorGuide.title: Mentor Guide — Day 4: Entrepreneur
mentorGuide.coachingObjective: Coach squads to draft a clear Unified Venture Proposition through Venture Design Studio.

timing.preSession: 10 min — review Venture Design Studio steps and observation form
timing.duringSession: Circulate during squad activities; coach at UVP (Step 3) transitions
timing.postSession: 15 min — complete observation forms and coaching notes

discussionQuestions: Who does your venture serve — be specific?
What problem are you solving and what outcome do they want?
Can you say your UVP in one sentence?

lookFor: Completes Venture Design Studio Step 3 synthesis
Engages in Venture Design Studio steps

warningSigns: Employee language — waiting to be told what to write

days.title: Day 5 — My Venture Direction
learningObjectives: Articulate 3-year venture vision with evidence from Week 1
Present Venture Portfolio in Day 5 review format
Commit to venture direction and Week 2 priorities
Complete Week 1 mentor coaching summary

decks.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — Week 1 Synthesis

slides.title: Day 5 — Week 1 Commitment Day
slides.summary: Five days ago you heard three stories. You built identity, discovered industry and customer, drafted your canvas. Today: present, commit, and launch Week 2.
discussionQuestions: What are you most proud of from Week 1?

slides.title: 3-Year Vision Review
slides.summary: Future self · Ambition · Impact · Career direction. Your presentation must show coherence — one venture story.
discussionQuestions: Does your portfolio tell one story?

slides.title: Opportunity Mapping
slides.summary: Connect persona problems → canvas priorities → Week 2 research. Venture entrepreneurs show evidence, not hope.
discussionQuestions: What evidence supports your direction?

slides.title: Hour 200 Venture Board Preview
slides.summary: Segment 1 ends at Hour 200 Venture Board. Week 1 is foundation — not the finish line. Today's presentation is practice for that gate.
discussionQuestions: What will Hour 200 look like for you?

slides.title: Deck 01 Close
slides.summary: Deck 02: presentation protocol and coaching summary.
discussionQuestions: Who presents first from your squad?

decks.title: Program Coach Deck 02 — Portfolio Presentation & Commitment

slides.title: Venture Portfolio Presentation Format
slides.summary: Use /my-venture-portfolio/present mode. Slides: Cover · Identity · Dream Board · Career · Canvas · Future Self · Commitment. 5–7 minutes per participant or squad representative.
slides.facilitatorNotes: Demo presentation mode if possible. 5 min.
discussionQuestions: Which section is your strongest?

slides.title: Presentation Rubric
slides.summary: Program Coach evaluates: identity, evidence, canvas, commitment, delivery. Mentors complete Week 1 coaching summary during presentations.
discussionQuestions: What will you improve before Hour 200?

slides.title: Squad Pitch Rehearsal
slides.summary: Squads rehearse 3-minute squad story: market + persona + direction.
discussionQuestions: What is your squad's unified thesis?

slides.title: Week 1 Commitment Ceremony
slides.summary: Each participant states one commitment aloud. Mentor records in coaching summary. Week 2 begins — industry execution, not more setup.
discussionQuestions: What commitment are you making today?

slides.title: Week 1 Close
slides.summary: ✓ Portfolio presented ✓ Blueprint Week 1 draft ✓ Mentor coaching summary ✓ Week 2 priority set Congratulations — you are venture builders.
discussionQuestions: What will you do in the next 24 hours?

sessionFlow: Open with Week 2 Activate hero — From Ideas to Evidence

sessionFlow.activity: Deck — What Did The Market Tell You?
sessionFlow.notes: Frame Tuesday: AM field research, PM market exchange.
SPIKE Studio Discover mode. Capture alias, occupation, 5 Q answers, reflection. AI extracts goals, pain points, themes.
7 min per squad. Each answers: What surprised you? Which assumption changed? What quote stayed with you? What will you investigate next?
SPIKE Studio auto-generates reflection prompt. Participants return to field with sharper hypotheses.

activities.title: Field Research — Discover Mode
instructions: Conduct customer interviews in the field
Encode each interview in SPIKE Studio (minimum 3, target 5)
Capture alias, occupation, and five question answers only

activities.title: Market Intelligence Exchange
instructions: Each squad shares: surprise, assumption change, memorable quote, next investigation
7 minutes per squad + 1 audience question
No PowerPoint required
debriefQuestions: What pattern are you beginning to notice?

prepChecklist: Confirm squads completed Day 6 prep (assumptions, 5 questions, field plan)
Brief mentors on Discover mode encoding in SPIKE Studio
Prepare Market Intelligence Exchange slide deck
Target: 3 interviews per member (9 per squad) — minimum 3 encoded to unlock exchange
debriefQuestions: Which squad showed the strongest interview evidence?
What patterns are emerging across Cassiopeia, Pegasus, and Argo Navis?
Are squads on track for 9 encoded interviews before Thursday FEC Lab?
commonPitfalls: Encoding without completing minimum 3 answer fields
Skipping Market Intelligence Exchange before reflection
coachingTips: One interview is a story — nine interviews are evidence
Celebrate customer quotes, not advisor opinions
Point squads back to SPIKE Studio — no duplicate forms

slides.title: From Venture Builder To Financial Entrepreneur
slides.summary: Discover ✓ Validate ✓ — today we shift toward Advise.

slides.title: Meet Miguel
slides.summary: Our client profile for the empathy workshop.

slides.title: The 5-Stage Client Journey
slides.summary: Awareness → Interest → Consideration → Decision → Success
slides.facilitatorNotes: Connect discovery work to where Miguel sits on the journey.

slides.title: From Understanding To Helping

slides.facilitatorNotes: Transition from researcher to future advisor.

slides.summary: 5 minutes per squad — present evidence, not ideas.

slides.summary: 24, Marketing Associate, Cebu — first job, dreams of financial independence.

slides.summary: Four steps: Observe → Listen → Understand → Connect

slides.title: What Would You Ask Miguel?
slides.summary: Before you advise, you must understand.

instructions: Frame the arc: Discover ✓ Validate ✓ → Advise

instructions: 5 minutes per squad — present evidence, not ideas

debriefQuestions: Which customer quote was most convincing?

activities.title: Empathy Lab — Think Like Miguel
instructions: Empathy In Action — four steps: Observe, Listen, Understand, Connect

Squad discussion — compare maps and share one insight
Reflect & share — connect to future advisor role
debriefQuestions: What surprised your squad about Miguel?

activities.title: Week 2 Stage Gate II Ceremony
instructions: Week 3 ADVISE preview (slides 11–14)

prepChecklist: Confirm squads completed FEC Validation Lab and pitch draft in SPIKE Studio

commonPitfalls: Skipping empathy workshop to extend pitch time
Pitching ideas instead of customer quotes and validated problems
Treating empathy map as a checklist instead of genuine perspective-taking
Individual grading instead of squad-first assessment
coachingTips: Use SPIKE presentation mode — deck aligns to pitch then workshop flow
After pitch block, return to slide 6 — do not skip the empathy lab
Stage gate is squad decision only — no individual scores
Empathy Lab saves automatically to portfolio

Is error log being used honestly?

days.title: Day 18 — VUL Concepts & Licensing Prep
learningObjectives: Explain VUL mechanics: protection, investment funds, charges
Compare Trad Life and VUL suitability factors without inappropriate recommendations
Complete VUL timed drill sets with error logging
Practice client-safe explanations of risk and fees

decks.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — VUL Mechanics
Program Coach Deck 02 — VUL Drill & Explanation Lab

sessionFlow.notes: Use diagrams; avoid return illustrations.
Mentors score clarity.

activities.title: VUL Timed Drill
instructions: Individual timed set
Log misses by domain

activities.title: Plain Language Explanation Lab
instructions: 60-sec explanations peer scored

activities.title: Fee Disclosure Roleplay
instructions: Advisor explains charges without minimizing

prepChecklist: Confirm fund fact sheet samples for study only
Reinforce no guaranteed return language
debriefQuestions: Who can explain NAVPU simply?
Any tendency to oversell investment upside?
Combined weak areas for mock exam day?
commonPitfalls: Confusing VUL with mutual funds only
coachingTips: Record exemplar 60-second explanations (with consent)

days.title: Day 19 — Mock Licensing Exams & Remediation

Analyze cohort weak domains for targeted remediation
Assign remediation dyads and tutoring plan
Confirm exam logistics and professional conduct expectations

learningObjectives: Present professional readiness portfolio including licensing progress
Complete 160-hour gate requirements
Articulate licensing pathway and Hour 110 milestone plan
Transition cohort mindset toward Week 5+ product and sales integration
